short: NVIDIA's high-speed interconnect that links GPUs directly, bypassing the slower PCIe bus.
definition: NVLink is NVIDIA's proprietary high-bandwidth, low-latency interconnect that lets GPUs exchange data directly with each other instead of going over the slower PCIe bus. Paired with NVSwitch fabric chips, it enables fast all-to-all communication across many GPUs in a rack. This makes it essential for tensor and pipeline parallelism, where many GPUs cooperate on one large model; each generation has raised bandwidth substantially.
